title: Naturalness Puzzle
description: A first-class node for a structural puzzle that the standard frameworks expose without resolving: a measured (or theoretically forced) number that has no natural explanation in the baseline theory. Puzzles are connected to data/ entries and benchmarks/ where applicable, and serve as targets for mechanisms/ to address.

id.description: Stable slug used as the filename.

kind.description: naturalness = an unprotected small or large number; pattern = a structured set of values without explanation in the baseline (mass hierarchies, mixing patterns); coincidence = numerical match between unrelated quantities; consistency = a theoretical-consistency demand (vacuum stability, anomaly cancellation) not yet derived from a deeper structure.

summary.description: Plain-English statement of the puzzle, 1-3 sentences.

characteristic_numbers.description: The 'ugly numbers' that motivate the puzzle. Each entry pairs the measured value with its naive theoretical expectation and the resulting gap.

gap_log10.description: Approximate log10 of value/expected (signed). Null when the gap is not numerically meaningful.

data_links.description: Optional list of data/<id> entries that bear directly on this puzzle.

benchmark_links.description: Optional list of benchmarks/<id> entries that bound this puzzle's parameters.

principles_stressed.description: Foundational principles this puzzle stresses. Same enum as schema/anomaly.schema.json.

history.description: Brief sketch of when the puzzle was articulated and how its framing has evolved.
